spec_match.rationale: Severe spec violation. Active CSS stylesheet defines progress() using invalid commas, e.g. progress(var(--current-step, 1), 1, var(--total-steps, 4)). Because Chrome 138 supports progress(), the JS fallback is disabled, but the browser discards the invalid CSS, leaving the progress bar entirely broken and invisible.

interactive.rationale: Fully interactive wizard with multiple flows and navigation buttons, though visually broken under native environments.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Perfect alignment with wizard onboarding design patterns.

edge_cases.rationale: Excellent design concept using custom properties.

references.rationale: Fails to include the mandatory spec or ChromeStatus entry reference links.

design_system.rationale: Adheres nicely to overall layout styles.

openQuestions.title: Fix critical syntax error in progress() CSS rules
openQuestions.detail: The active CSS stylesheet (lines 73-77 and 152-156) defines progress() using invalid commas: `progress(var(--current-step, 1), 1, var(--total-steps, 4))`. In Chrome 138+, the browser supports progress(), which disables the JS fallback. However, because of the comma syntax error, the browser discards the CSS rules, resulting in the progress bars remaining completely blank and invisible. Please change the syntax to use standard keywords: `progress(var(--current-step, 1) from 1 to var(--total-steps, 4))`.

openQuestions.title: Add mandatory chromestatus.com spec reference link
openQuestions.detail: The page is missing the mandatory reference link to the ChromeStatus entry. Please add a references section containing a link to `https://chromestatus.com/feature/5112558941634560`.

summary: An exceptionally engaging step-by-step wizard flows indicator dashboard. However, it suffers from a critical CSS syntax error: it uses obsolete comma separation in progress() instead of 'from ... to ...', causing the layout to break and stay completely invisible on modern supported browsers.
